tools.name: task_save
tools.description: Save the current conversation as a reusable task template (definition). This is NOT for adding items to the user's task queue — use task_list_add for that. task_save extracts the conversation pattern into a reusable definition with placeholders that can be run later with different inputs.

conversation_id.description: The conversation to capture as a task template. If omitted, uses the current conversation.

title.description: Optional override for the auto-generated task title

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: task_run
tools.description: Run a task template. Resolves the template by name (fuzzy match) or ID, renders it with the provided inputs, and returns the rendered prompt for execution as a Task (work item).

task_name.description: Fuzzy match a task template by name (case-insensitive substring match)

task_id.description: Exact match a task template by ID

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: task_list
tools.description: List saved task templates (reusable definitions). To see your active Tasks (work items), use task_list_show instead.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: task_delete
tools.description: Delete one or more task templates by ID. Also removes associated task runs and work items (Tasks).

task_ids.description: One or more task IDs to delete.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: task_list_show
tools.description: List the user's Task Queue (work items) with their status, priority, and last run info. Use this when the user says "show my tasks", "what's in my queue", "what's on my task list", or similar. Present the results as a summary in the conversation.

status.description: Optional status filter. A single status string (e.g. "queued") or an array of statuses to include.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: task_list_add
tools.description: Add a task to the user's Task Queue. Use this when the user says "add to my tasks", "add to my queue", "put this on my task list", "track this task", or any variation of adding a one-off item they want to remember or work on. You can provide just a title for ad-hoc items, or reference an existing task definition by name or ID. Do NOT use schedule_create or reminder for simple "add to tasks" requests — those are for timed/recurring automation only.

task_id.description: ID of an existing task definition to enqueue. Provide this, task_name, or just title.

task_name.description: Title/name of an existing task definition to search for (case-insensitive substring match). Provide this, task_id, or just title.

title.description: Title for the work item. When provided WITHOUT task_id or task_name, creates an ad-hoc work item directly (a lightweight task template is auto-created behind the scenes). When provided WITH task_id or task_name, overrides the task definition title.

execution_prompt.description: The full instruction to execute when this task runs. If omitted, the title is used as the execution prompt. Use this to preserve detailed instructions (e.g. full file paths, specific parameters) that would be lost in a short display title.

notes.description: Notes to attach to the work item.

sort_index.description: Manual sort order within the priority tier.

if_exists.description: What to do if an active work item with the same title already exists. Defaults to "reuse_existing".

required_tools.description: Tools the task will need at execution time. Always specify this — think about what tools are needed to accomplish the task (e.g. ["host_bash"] for shell commands, ["host_file_read", "host_file_write"] for file operations, ["web_search"] for web lookups). The user must approve these before the task runs. Pass [] if no tools are needed.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: task_list_update
tools.description: Update an existing task in the Task Queue. Can change priority, notes, status (including marking as done), or sort order. Identifies the task by work item ID, task ID, task name, or title.

work_item_id.description: Direct work item ID (most precise selector)

task_id.description: Task definition ID to find the work item for

task_name.description: Task name/title to search for (case-insensitive exact match)

title.description: Work item title to search for (case-insensitive exact match)

notes.description: Updated notes for the work item

status.description: New status for the work item. To mark a task as done, it must currently be in 'awaiting_review' status.

sort_index.description: Manual sort order within the same priority tier

filter_priority_tier.description: Disambiguation filter: narrow by current priority tier (0=high, 1=medium, 2=low) when multiple items share the same title/task_id. This identifies WHICH item to update — it is NOT the new priority value.

filter_status.description: Disambiguation filter: narrow by current status when multiple items share the same title/task_id.

created_order.description: Disambiguation filter: pick the Nth oldest match (1 = oldest, 2 = second oldest, etc.) when multiple items share the same title/task_id.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: task_list_remove
tools.description: Remove a task from the Task Queue. Identifies the task by work item ID, task ID, task name, or title. When multiple items match, use the disambiguation fields (priority_tier, status, created_order) to narrow down.

work_item_id.description: Direct work item ID (most precise selector)

task_id.description: Task definition ID to find the work item for

task_name.description: Task name/title to search for (case-insensitive exact match)

title.description: Work item title to search for (case-insensitive exact match)

priority_tier.description: Disambiguator: filter by priority tier (0 = high, 1 = medium, 2 = low)

status.description: Disambiguator: filter by work item status

created_order.description: Disambiguator: 1-indexed creation order among matches (1 = oldest, 2 = second oldest, etc.)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: task_queue_run
tools.description: Run a task from the Task Queue in the background. Use this when the user says "run this task", "execute this task", "start this task", or wants to kick off a queued work item. The task runs asynchronously — the user can continue chatting while it executes. Required tool permissions are auto-approved since the user is explicitly requesting execution.

work_item_id.description: Direct work item ID (most precise selector)

task_name.description: Task name/title to search for (case-insensitive substring match)

title.description: Work item title to search for (case-insensitive substring match)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called
